text: For pencil sketching, start with light strokes and build up layers gradually.
Charcoal drawings benefit from blending with a tortillon for smooth shadows.
Watercolor works best on 300gsm cold press paper to avoid warping.
Ink drawings require confident, single strokes — avoid sketching with ink.
For portraits, focus on the distance between eyes — it equals the width of one eye.
Hatching and cross-hatching are great for adding value in pen drawings.
Digital art beginners should learn brush opacity and pressure sensitivity first.
Use the negative space around a subject to check proportions.
For shading spheres, identify your light source and place your darkest tone opposite it.
Gesture drawing practice for 5-10 minutes daily improves figure proportions rapidly.
